historicalScope: Ancient Memphis, Early Dynastic / Old Kingdom, Kom el-Fakhry / Mit Rahina settlement evidence with early Ptah / Hwt-ka-Ptah endpoint.
qualityTarget: Cinematic eye-height hero street benchmark for material, ground, plaster, mudbrick, contact-shadow, haze, and color-grading passes.

shots.label: Hero Street Main
notes: Primary eye-height first-person composition down the dense residential lane.
Use this shot for before/after decisions during texture, material, baked-light, haze, and color passes.
Do not move this camera unless the user explicitly approves a new canonical benchmark.

shots.label: Street Entry
notes: Diagnostic view from the Nile/threshold approach into the offset residential street.
Use to verify the district plan still feels indirect and not perfectly aligned to the gate.

shots.label: Hero Street Material Close-Up
notes: Diagnostic close material read for packed dust, plaster, mudbrick, pottery, linen, and contact shadows.
Use after atlas or baked-light changes to catch tiling, banding, or over-bright haze.

acceptanceChecks: The canonical shot is captured at 1280x720 with chrome=0.
The camera position and lookAt values match this lock file.
The district layout is not moved while judging material quality.
The street no longer reads as tiled or banded from the canonical shot.
Doorways and wall bases show credible baked-looking darkness.
The palette stays historically restrained: warm sun, cooler shadow, darker dirt, pale linen, clay pottery, dusty plaster, mudbrick.
